MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 19, 2025, in Department 304, Line 1.
No appearances, not contested, The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Irwin Industries, Inc's ("Defendant" or "Irwin") Motion for Summary Judgment is OFF CALENDAR. Opposition filed. Reply filed.

Defendant failed to give sufficient notice pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure
section 437c(a)(2). Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(2) provides that "notice of the motion and supporting papers shall be served on all other parties to the action at least 81 days before the time appointed for hearing." If the notice is served electronically, the required 81-day period of notice must be increased by two court days. (Code Civ. Proc., 437c(a)(2) and 1010.6(a)(4)(B); see Cole v. Superior Court (2022) 87 Cal.App.5th 84, 87-88.) The Court cannot shorten the notice period without the parties' prior consent. (Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 758, 764; McMahon v. Super. Ct. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 112, 117-118.) Nor can the Court cure the defect by continuing the hearing by the missing number of days. (Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1267-1268.) The notice period is mandatory. (Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1267-68.)
Defendant filed this motion for summary judgment on May 30, 2025, 81 days prior to the hearing date of August 19, 2025. The proof of service shows the motion was e-served on the same date. However, because Defendant chose to serve the motion electronically, it was required to serve the motion two court days earlier, by May 28, 2025. Defendant's motion was therefore two days late.
In reply, Defendant contends that Plaintiffs do not claim prejudice from the two-day delay and note that Plaintiffs stipulated with a different defendant to accept a shortened notice period. (Reply, 2-4.) Moreover, Defendant cites a footnote in a recent Court of Appeal case, CFP BDA LLC v. Superior Court of Riverside County (2025) 112 Cal.App.5th 1006 ("CFP"), to argue it timely filed its motion despite serving it electronically on the last day personal service was required. (Reply, 3.) The Court finds the arguments unpersuasive.

(mini-minutes 1 of 2 continues, see next entry)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2025 Off Calendar

DEFENDANT IRWIN INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTOIN FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - FOR TENTATIVE RULING PURPOSES ONLY
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (mini-minutes 2 of 2)

In CFP, the Court of Appeal held that timely motions for summary judgments must be heard regardless of local rules or calendaring issues. In that case, petitioners' motion for summary judgment had been rejected by the trial court because the moving papers were not filed within ten court days after a reservation was made according to a local rule. (CFP, supra, 112 Cal.App.5th 1006.) The court held that "given that Local Rule 3310 was developed to manage an impacted court calendar, and it has the practical effect of requiring litigants to prepare and file summary judgment motions weeks or months earlier than statutorily required in order to reserve a timely hearing date, we see no basis for distinguishing this case from Cole or Sentry." (Id.) The court further noted that the motion for summary judgment was not untimely because petitioners emailed respondents indicating their intent to deliver the motion by courier unless respondents agreed to accept electronic service in lieu of personal service, which they did. "Had [respondents] not agreed to accept the MSJ via email, it would have been delivered personally on the due date. Thus, we do not consider service to be untimely." (Id. at 5 n.2.)
Here, the factual circumstances in CFP are not present. Defendant admits in its reply that it "did not expressly ask Plaintiffs to accept electronic service" in lieu of personal service. (Reply, 3.) Moreover, unlike CFP, the Court is not concerned with compliance with local procedural requirements, but with a statutory notice period governing motions for summary judgment. The notice requirement under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(2) is mandatory, and noncompliance cannot be excused merely because the delay is brief.
Accordingly, Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment was not timely served and must therefore be taken off calendar. Order signed in open court. = (EPS/304).

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-12-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via CourtCall:
John Flynn of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG LLP, 213.622.7313, appearing for defendant Hajoca Corporation;
Nona Sarkisyan of BERKES CRANE SANTANA & SPANGLER LLP, 213.955.1150, appearing for defendant Fluor Corporation;
Melissa Berberoglu of POLSINELLI PC, 310.556.1150, appearing for defendant McMaster-Carr Supply Company;
Justin Sha of BUTY & CURLIANO LLP, 510.267.3000, appearing for defendant Petrochem Insulation, Inc. fka Petro-Chemical Insulation Inc.;
Doris Youmara of CMBG3 LAW LLC, 949.467.9500, appearing for defendant Pepboys and William Powell Company.

Not contested, not argued, and the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED, with the following admonishment. No opposition filed.

Despite requesting the Court to add decedent's legal heirs as plaintiffs to the case, no one is ever identified by name in either the Notice, MPA, or counsel's declaration. The only way to identify who Plaintiff seeks to add is by referencing the proposed FAC and the proposed order. The second deficiency is more significant. Based on the FAC, and only the FAC, Plaintiff appears to want to add Nicholas Zaragoza-also a legal heir-as a nominal defendant. While this may be permissible under CCP Section 382, his addition as a defendant does not appear anywhere in the moving paers; nor does any mention of CCP Section 382. Counsel is advised that the Court will not extend this courtesy in the future for an otherwise deficient motion, opposed or unopposed.

IT IS ORDERED that DALE D. SPURRIER (dba California Boiler), previously sued as Does 5 and 302; and WESTERN MACARTHUR COMPANY, previously sued as Does 6 and 303, are to be added as defendants to this action. NICHOLAS ZARAGOZA is added as a nominal defendant pursuant to CCP Section 382. Plaintiff shall file and serve the First Amended Complaint on the newly added defendants forthwith. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served on all Defendants who have previously appeared in the action. It is further ordered that legal heirs NATALIE ZARAGOZA and ALEXANDRA ZARAGOZA shall be added as plaintiffs to this action.

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Not Present. Matter not reported. (301/RCE)
